markets.question: Will Russia enter Zaporizhia by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of the city of Zaporizhia (47.8423587068344° N, 35.15074278438932° E; https://maps.app.goo.gl/Gezpp2b4SDnefJ5s9) by December 31, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET.

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the city or settlement is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

markets.question: Will Cisco's Networking revenue be above $8.5B in Q3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Cisco's Networking revenue for the third fiscal quarter of 2026, as reported in its official company earnings materials, is above the listed amount. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The specified metric will be considered as reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Subsequent revisions will not be considered.

If the specified company’s official earnings materials for the specified quarter are released, and the specified metric is not included, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the specified company does not release quarterly earnings materials for the specified quarter by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the specified metric is reported as a range rather than a specific number, the midpoint of the range will be used for resolution of this market.

The resolution source for this market is Cisco's official company earnings materials, including press releases, investor presentations, and regulatory filings. If the specified metric is not reported in these materials, recordings or transcripts of the company’s earnings webcast may also be used.

Note: This market will resolve based on the most numerically precise version of the specified metric reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Only the specified metric will be considered; alternate versions that differ in definition or scope from the specified metric will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cisco's networking segment revenue for fiscal Q3 2026 (ended April 25) commands trader attention ahead of the May 13 earnings release and conference call, building on Q2's robust 21% year-over-year surge to $8.3 billion driven by hyperscaler AI infrastructure demand, campus refresh cycles, and Silicon One switch traction. Company guidance from February pegged total Q3 revenue at $15.4-15.6 billion (consensus $15.56 billion) and non-GAAP EPS at $1.02-1.04, with product orders reflecting sustained AI momentum despite elevated memory costs pressuring gross margins. Key risks include supply chain bottlenecks for 800G/1.6T optics; Splunk integration progress and full-year revenue outlook ($61.2-61.7 billion) will shape post-earnings sentiment shifts.

events.title: CA Osasuna vs. Club Atlético de Madrid
events.description: This event is for the upcoming La Liga game, scheduled for Tuesday, May 12, 2026 between CA Osasuna and Club Atlético de Madrid.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Osasuna at 37.5%, Atletico Madrid at 35.5%, and draw at 26.5%, reflecting a tightly contested La Liga Matchday 36 clash at El Sadar driven by Atletico's deepening injury crisis—now eight players sidelined, including fresh blows to José Giménez (high-grade ankle sprain) and Nahuel Molina (thigh muscle injury) announced May 11—offsetting their fourth-place standing and historical dominance (10 straight La Liga wins over Osasuna prior to recent slips). Osasuna, 10th with 42 points, leverages strong home form despite suspending goalkeeper Sergio Herrera and missing Víctor Muñoz (calf), following a 3-2 loss to Levante; both sides enter off defeats, with Atletico's poor away record amplifying the home edge and keeping probabilities bunched.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket pins Israel at 35% and Greece at 26% to claim the Eurovision 2026 televote crown in Vienna's Grand Final, reflecting a nail-biter atop closely contested dynamics shaped by first dress rehearsals and audience polls from Semi-Final 1 today. Israel's Noam Bettan surges with "Michelle," a multilingual ballad fueling diaspora voting blocs—historically potent in public tallies—bolstered by powerful staging previews amid geopolitical buzz. Greece's Akylas counters fiercely via "Ferto," a hyper-techno banger with addictive hooks and anti-consumerism lyrics exploding on socials for pan-European televote pull. Romania's edgy "Choke Me" and Finland's violin-driven "Liekinheitin" lurk as dark horses, but Israel's bloc strength edges Greece's viral momentum; Semi-Final qualifiers and final run-throughs could flip the script before Saturday's vote.

markets.question: Will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit (LOW) $3.50 Week of May 11 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 11 2026, any 1-minute candle for Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Slovenia's March 2026 parliamentary election, where Robert Golob's Freedom Movement narrowly led but failed to secure a coalition by April 20, President Nataša Pirc Musar opened negotiations to Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS), which secured the second-most seats. Advanced coalition talks among SDS, Demokrati, and the NSi–SLS–Fokus alliance have reached 95% agreement as of May 12, positioning Janša for imminent nomination as prime minister-designate and a National Assembly confirmation vote. Trader consensus at 92% reflects this momentum and skin-in-the-game assessment of center-right majority potential, though scenarios like final negotiation breakdowns, alternative left-leaning coalitions, or a failed confidence vote could challenge the outcome.

events.title: NBA Playoffs: Finals MVP
events.description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 NBA Finals Most Valuable Player award for the 2026 NBA Playoffs.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by NBA rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 NBA Playoffs are cancelled, postponed after June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NBA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Shai Gilgeous-Alexander as the clear Finals MVP favorite at 56% implied probability after the Thunder swept the Lakers 4-0 in the Western Conference semifinals, capped by his 35 points and 8 assists in the clincher to advance to the conference finals for the second straight year. Victor Wembanyama follows at 18.5%, buoyed by his playoff dominance for the Spurs—including a 39-point outburst and 12 blocks in their ongoing semifinal series against the Timberwolves despite a recent ejection—positioning San Antonio as the top West challenger. Jalen Brunson's 12.5% share reflects the Knicks' commanding 4-0 sweep of the 76ers, fueled by his 33-point Game 3, as New York eyes the Eastern Conference finals against Detroit. OKC's championship edge and Shai's reigning MVP form drive the market skew.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Goldman Sachs at 53.5% implied probability to serve as lead underwriter for SpaceX's anticipated "Project Apex" IPO, targeting a late June debut with a potential $1.75 trillion valuation, driven by recent reports highlighting banker Kim Posnett's prominent role alongside Morgan Stanley's Michael Grimes, who has deep ties to Elon Musk from the Tesla IPO. Morgan Stanley trails closely at 35.5%, bolstered by its historical advisory position and recent syndicate inclusion with Bank of America (10.3%), JPMorgan, and Citigroup as senior banks in a 21-firm lineup confirmed after SpaceX's confidential filing in early April. Bank of America's edge stems from early Financial Times sourcing, while others lag due to junior roles. Upcoming early June roadshows could solidify the lead left amid accelerating Starlink growth and massive capital needs for Mars ambitions.

markets.question: Exact Score: Manchester City FC 2 - 1 Crystal Palace FC?
markets.description: In the upcoming Premier League game between Manchester City FC and Crystal Palace FC, scheduled for May 13, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the Manchester City FC vs. Crystal Palace FC match originally scheduled for May 13, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market will be the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if final official statistics are not published within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Uncle" or "Unc" this week?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 11, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 17, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's upcoming National Jubilee of Prayer, Praise, and Thanksgiving on May 17 at the National Mall—rededicating the U.S. as "one nation under God" ahead of the 250th anniversary—anchors trader focus, with his planned scriptural reading from 2 Chronicles 7:14 and major address likely driving verbal mentions of signature themes like national renewal. Recent criticisms of the fragile Iran ceasefire, Cuba talks signaling potential regime shifts, North Korea references, and economic pushes on rare earth minerals, shipbuilding, and tech including NVIDIA's Blackwell amid China tensions have boosted consensus on foreign policy terms. Domestic priorities like midterms, transgender issues, and wind energy critiques, plus prior administration jabs, shape expectations for public speeches and events through Sunday's deadline.

markets.question: Will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit (HIGH) $480 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Tesla shares hovered around $431 on May 12, down 3% intraday from the prior close of $445, reflecting trader caution amid robotaxi rollout challenges in Texas, where long wait times have tempered initial enthusiasm despite expansion plans to nine cities by July. Q1 2026 earnings released April 22 exceeded forecasts with adjusted EPS of $0.41 versus $0.30 expected and revenue rising 16% year-over-year to $22.4 billion, driven by energy storage growth offsetting softer vehicle deliveries—the weakest quarterly figure in a year. Analyst consensus price target stands near $400, with valuations hinging on full self-driving progress, Optimus robot commercialization, and AI capex amid EV market competition. Q2 production and delivery data due early July looms as the next key catalyst for May price action.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Karachi be 27°C or below on May 13?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Masroor Airbase Station in degrees Celsius on 13 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Masroor Airbase Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pk/karachi/OPKC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies a near-even split between 37°C or higher (49.5%) and exactly 36°C (38.5%) as the highest temperature in Karachi on May 13, reflecting Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) forecasts of 35–37°C maximums under hot, humid conditions with active heatwave alerts in interior Sindh. This positioning stems from an early-May heatwave that peaked at 44.1°C on May 4—the city's hottest day since 2018—followed by moderation to mid-30s°C amid sea breeze influences from the Arabian Sea, but now with subsidence from high-pressure systems driving a slight uptick. Climatologically, May averages 35°C highs with peaks rarely exceeding 38°C; however, model spreads show international guidance like AccuWeather projecting 38–39°C (100–102°F), introducing uncertainty from variable coastal moderation and low humidity. PMD's evening update will refine resolution based on official airport station data.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the AL-07 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the AL-07 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Terri Sewell's uncontested Democratic primary—canceled due to no challengers—combined with no Republican candidates filing for their May 19 primary have entrenched trader consensus at 79.5% for a Democratic victory in the Black-majority AL-07, a district she has held since 2010 with margins exceeding 60% even against GOP opponents. Recent Alabama legislative action in a special session following a Supreme Court ruling lifted a prior injunction on congressional maps preserved the district's strong Democratic lean in the Black Belt region, including Selma and parts of Birmingham and Tuscaloosa. Sewell's robust fundraising ($3.6 million cash on hand) and fresh endorsements, like from AFGE, reinforce her incumbency advantage ahead of the November 3 general election, though independents could file by primary day.

markets.question: Will Robert Neuman win the 2026 Oregon Governor Republican primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Oregon, scheduled to take place on May 19, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Oregon Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Oregon Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Oregon Governor Republican Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Oregon, scheduled to take place on May 19, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Oregon Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Oregon Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Christine Drazan commands trader consensus at 87.5% implied probability to win Oregon's Republican gubernatorial primary on May 19, driven by consistent late-April polls showing her lead among likely voters—31-35% in Nelson Research (April 14-17) and Hoffman Research (April 23-24) surveys of 515-620 respondents, ahead of Ed Diehl (16-18%) and Chris Dudley (14-15%), with 25-30% undecided leaning her way on forced choice. Her edge stems from top name recognition as the 2022 GOP nominee who narrowly lost the general election. A May 5 debate underscored shared criticisms of Gov. Tina Kotek but no challengers gained traction, as ballots mailed late April enter final voting amid closed primary dynamics. Late momentum or turnout surprises could narrow the gap.

markets.question: Will "Lord of the Flies" be the #2 global Netflix show this week?
markets.description:
Netflix is expected to update its global Top 10 TV shows list on top10.netflix.com on Tuesday, May 12, 2026, 3:00 PM ET, reflecting viewership from the previous week (Monday to Sunday).

This market will resolve based on which show this update ranks as the #2 global Netflix show.

The ranking is based on total views globally, as reported by Netflix for TV shows (English only).

If the top10.netflix.com update does not occur by May 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

events.title: What will be the #2 global Netflix show this week?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors "Should I Marry A Murderer?" as the #2 global Netflix show this week, with a 95.1% implied probability, driven by its commanding position in Netflix's latest weekly Top 10 rankings—10.6 million views, trailing only "Man on Fire" at 11 million while lapping #3 "Unchosen" at 8.8 million. This true crime limited series, which premiered late April, has fueled binge-watching frenzy through its stranger-than-fiction tale of a pathologist engaged to a murder suspect, amplified by viral social buzz and strong non-English market performance. The 1.8 million view gap to challengers solidifies its frontrunner status as the week nears close. Realistic upsets would require an improbable late surge from "Unchosen" or "Running Point," or an unforeseen drop-off in completion rates, though historical streaming trajectories suggest stability. Final rankings lock Tuesday.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli leads trader consensus at 67% implied probability for the FIA Action of the Year award following his daring double overtake on Lando Norris and Max Verstappen into Turn 11 on Laps 28 and 29 at the Miami Grand Prix, securing the race lead en route to his third consecutive victory after China and Japan. Verstappen sits second at 27.5% after an astonishing recovery from a Lap 1 360-degree spin amid opening-lap chaos, demonstrating elite car control to charge back to fifth. Lance Stroll and Sergio Perez round out notable contenders at 16.3% and 15.8%, buoyed by bold midfield moves in prior rounds amid the 2026 regulations' surge in overtakes, though Miami developments heavily favor the frontrunners.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Rep. Glenn Grothman's strong incumbency advantage and superior fundraising—$700,000 cash on hand versus challengers' minimal totals—bolster trader consensus pricing Republicans at 82.5% to retain Wisconsin's 6th Congressional District, rated Solid Republican by Cook Political Report, Inside Elections, and Sabato's Crystal Ball amid its R+8 partisan voter index. The crowded Democratic primary field of eight candidates, lacking a clear frontrunner, fragments opposition ahead of the August 11 primaries, while independents like firefighter Mike Thurow pose negligible threats in this reliably GOP stronghold, where Grothman won by over 20 points in 2024. No major developments in the past 30 days have altered this structural edge, with nomination signature deadlines looming June 1.

markets.question: Will Mistral have the best Coding AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

Realistic challenges include surprise OpenAI or Google releases surpassing benchmarks or SWE-bench's pivot to multilingual evaluations.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AAPL%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Hong Kong be 23°C on May 14?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 14 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Min (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's latest 9-day forecast, updated May 11, implies a minimum temperature of 24°C on May 14 amid cloudy skies, heavy showers, and squally thunderstorms driven by an approaching trough of low pressure, with south to southwest winds at force 4–5 enhancing moisture influx. Trader sentiment splits nearly evenly at 50% for 27°C or higher versus 49.5% for 17°C or below, reflecting model uncertainty over whether persistent cloud cover and high humidity (80–95%) will trap heat for warmer overnight lows or if upper-air disturbances usher cooler air advection akin to recent May means of 22.7°C through May 10. Mid-range outcomes like 24–26°C hover at 46–47%, hinging on trough timing; watch tomorrow's midday update and Tropical Cyclone Hagupit influences for shifts.

markets.question: Will there be no Somaliland parliamentary election before 2027?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Somaliland in late March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Somaliland House of Representatives (Golaha Wakiilada) election. 

If no election takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No election before 2027".

If an election takes place but the results are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will remain open until the certified results are released. If the results are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Somaliland Parliament. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Somaliland government, specifically the Somaliland National Electoral Commission (Komishanka Doorashooyinka Qaranka) (slnec.net).

President Irro has 21 days to promulgate the extension or it auto-approves, with potential legal challenges looming.

Strong legs through spring break and word-of-mouth have widened the gap over competitors like indie limited-release Mother Mary ($2.5 million) and niche titles such as You, Me & Tuscany ($19 million). markets.question: Will Google have the #2 AI model at the end of June 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the second-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the TX-38 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the TX-38 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Ukraine agrees not to join NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Ukraine publicly agrees not to join NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An official pledge by Ukraine not to join NATO will qualify for a “Yes” resolution whether as a unilateral announcement or part of an agreement with the Russian Federation.

Any agreement or pledge made before the resolution date of this market will qualify, regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

An agreement by Ukraine not to join NATO for any amount of time will count (e.g. If Ukraine not to join NATO for 10 years this will qualify).

An agreement by Ukraine not to join NATO as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not finalized or part of a formalized peace deal. The September 8, 1995 “Agreed Basic Principles” between Bosnia and Yugoslavia which recognized the borders and sovereignty of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and was later formalized through the Dayton Peace Agreement is an example of a qualifying agreement. 

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by the Ukraine, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming a qualifying agreement has been reached will also count.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 78% chance against Ukraine agreeing to forgo NATO membership before 2027, driven by stalled U.S.-mediated peace talks with Russia despite proposals from the Trump administration calling for a NATO moratorium, military caps, and territorial concessions. Kyiv rejected key elements in early 2026 Geneva rounds, prioritizing constitutional NATO aspirations and Western security guarantees over formal renunciation, as reiterated by President Zelenskyy amid no consensus among NATO allies on accession timelines. Recent signals like President Putin's May 10 statement hinting at war's end lack specifics on NATO clauses, while ongoing diplomatic pauses and unresolved territorial disputes reinforce doubts about any binding pre-2027 agreement.

markets.question: Will Racing Bulls get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the constructor team that achieves pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix, scheduled for May 23, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the constructor team that is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a constructor team sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that constructor team.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

markets.question: Will Nicolae Ciucă be the next Prime Minister of Romania?
markets.description:

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed by the President of Romania and receive a vote of confidence from Parliament, resulting in the official formation of a new government. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister who does not receive a parliamentary vote of confidence will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister takes office by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Romania; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will FC Baník Ostrava win on 2026-05-12?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 12, 2026
If FC Baník Ostrava wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: 1. FC Slovácko vs. FC Baník Ostrava
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Czechia Fortuna Liga game, scheduled for Tuesday, May 12, 2026 between 1. FC Slovácko and FC Baník Ostrava.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin contest in this Czech First League relegation group clash, with Slovácko holding a slim home edge at 49.5% implied probability amid evenly matched records after 30 matches—Slovácko's 5-8-17 yielding 23 points, Baník Ostrava's 5-7-18 at 22. Recent form underscores the parity: Slovácko's poor home ledger (4 wins, 3 draws, 8 losses) meets Baník's dismal away struggles, tempered by their February 2-2 draw at this venue. Baník enters shorthanded, missing injured Filip Kubala, David Látal, Michal Kohút, and Srdjan Plavšić (hand surgery), plus recent suspension concerns, yet traders see upset potential in the visitors' resilience against top sides like Plzeň last weekend.

markets.question: Valencia: Completed Match: Pablo Carreno Busta vs Francisco Comesana
markets.description: This market refers to the tennis match between Pablo Carreno Busta and Francisco Comesana in the Valencia, originally scheduled for May 12, 2026 at 10:00AM ET. This market will resolve to "Yes" if all games and sets required to determine a match winner under governing body or event organizer rules are played to completion through normal play. Otherwise, if the match is not completed for any reason, it will resolve to "No." If a forfeit of any kind occurs, including but not limited to a walkover or retirement, this market will resolve "No." If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to "No." The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Valencia: Pablo Carreno Busta vs Francisco Comesana
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Pablo Carreno Busta and Francisco Comesana in the Valencia, originally scheduled for May 12, 2026 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Pablo Carreno Busta' if Pablo Carreno Busta advances against Francisco Comesana.

This market will resolve to 'Francisco Comesana' if Francisco Comesana advances against Pablo Carreno Busta.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pablo Carreño Busta enters as the slight trader consensus favorite against Francisco Comesaña in the Valencia Challenger first-round clash on outdoor clay, buoyed by a 1-0 head-to-head lead from their 2025 Roland Garros encounter (6-2, 7-6(3), 7-5). The 34-year-old Spaniard, ranked No. 91, leverages home-crowd support and recent clay momentum, including an Alicante Challenger final appearance and a Murcia title earlier this year, despite a straight-sets Rome Masters loss to Alejandro Tabilo after qualifying. Comesaña (No. 114), 25, counters with fresher qualifier wins over Billy Harris and Leandro Riedi in Rome before falling to Jan-Lennard Struff, though his 3-10 YTD record tempers expectations amid both players' injury-free status.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: The Pentagon's May 1 announcement of withdrawing 5,000 US troops from Germany over the next 6-12 months, amid tensions over ally support during recent Iran-related strains, has intensified debate on America's NATO commitment but failed to signal full withdrawal, keeping trader consensus odds low at 8% for exit by December 31. President Trump's April Truth Social post criticizing NATO for lacking reciprocity echoes long-standing demands for higher European defense spending, yet Article 13 requires one-year notice post-denunciation, complicated by 2023 congressional legislation mandating Senate supermajority approval. Frontline NATO states like Poland and the Baltics are repositioning for relocated forces, while a 2026 NATO summit looms as a potential flashpoint for further diplomatic signals or escalations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Al Nassr holds an eight-point lead atop the Saudi Pro League table over unbeaten Al Hilal after 29 matches, with six rounds left, setting up a potential title-clinching Riyadh Derby at Al-Awwal Park on May 7. Al Nassr's recent form shines with victories like 1-0 at Al Ettifaq on April 15 and 5-2 over Al Najma, bolstered by Cristiano Ronaldo's fitness post-hamstring recovery, though midfielder Angelo remains sidelined by muscle pain. markets.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent May 2026 polls from Futura Inteligência and Ideia show Senator Flávio Bolsonaro slightly ahead of incumbent President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva in simulated second-round runoffs—46.9% to 44.4% and 45.3% to 44.7%, respectively—driving Polymarket traders' consensus favoring Flávio at 43.4% implied probability over Lula's 39.5% for the October 4 first-round vote. First-round surveys depict a fragmented field with both leaders around 37-40%, trailed by Ronaldo Caiado (5-6%) and Romeu Zema (3-4%), virtually ensuring a runoff under Brazil's two-round system. The race remains tight due to Lula's dominance in the Northeast offsetting Flávio's strength in São Paulo and the South, alongside high rejection rates (Lula 47%, Flávio 44%) and economic pressures on the administration. Separation could arise from right-wing consolidation, fresh economic data, scandals, or Lula's health amid his age of 80.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Seoul be 26°C on May 14?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 14 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Incheon Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

Traders await KMA's next short-term update tomorrow for refined guidance on exact thresholds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the Wisconsin Democratic gubernatorial primary favors former Lt. Gov. Lt. Gov. Sara Rodriguez and Milwaukee Mayor David Crowley lag on electability concerns raised in April forums.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest National Weather Service guidance and numerical models like GFS and ECMWF project Atlanta's highest temperature on May 13 clustering in the upper 70s to low 80s°F, driving the tight trader consensus with 80-81°F (35.5%) edging 78-79°F (31.5%). Post-frontal northerly winds at 5-10 mph and lingering mid-level clouds from today's system introduce uncertainty in peak diurnal heating and boundary layer mixing, potentially capping highs below 80°F if clearing delays, or allowing 82-83°F (17%) under fuller sunshine. Climatological May averages near 80°F align with this range, but model ensembles show spread: GFS slightly warmer, ECMWF cooler with higher cloud persistence. Evening 00Z runs and morning soundings could shift implied probabilities before resolution at Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport.

markets.question: Will South Korea ETF (EWY) hit (LOW) $140 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for South Korea ETF (EWY) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the South Korea ETF (EWY) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.EWY%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.EWY%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

McIlroy's visible limp during Sunday's Truist Championship final round sparked injury concerns just days before tee times, while DeChambeau admitted left shoulder discomfort and Collin Morikawa withdrew from that event amid ongoing health issues—key developments tempering sentiment on their top-5 prospects. Scheffler's consistent ball-striking suits Aronimink's demanding length and 2018 BMW Championship layout, where Keegan Bradley prevailed in a playoff, but weather and late withdrawals remain wild cards in this deep major field.

markets.question: Will Lionel Messi be the top goalscorer at the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the player who scores the most goals through all main tournament rounds of the 2026 FIFA World Cup competition.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by FIFA rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the player who scored fewer goals from penalty kicks. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after August 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is no leader declared within that timeframe, the corresponding market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: NY-12 Democratic Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the NY-12 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 23, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the closely contested NY-12 Democratic primary to replace retiring Rep. Jerry Nadler on June 23, trader consensus favors state Assemblymember Alex Bores at 47% implied probability, propelled by recent endorsements from Rep. Pat Ryan, the United Federation of Teachers, and a $3.5 million infusion from a crypto billionaire, alongside his internal polling strength and first campaign ad launch. Challenger Micah Lasher holds at 36.5% with Gov. Kathy Hochul's backing and ties as Nadler's protégé, while Jack Schlossberg trails at 16.5% buoyed by Kennedy name recognition and youth appeal from social media but criticized for inexperience in recent forums. This affluent Manhattan district's dynamics hinge on union support, fundraising, and turnout, with early voting starting June 13.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 72.5% implied probability against the U.S. enacting a comprehensive federal AI safety bill before 2027, driven by persistent congressional gridlock in the 119th Congress amid partisan divides over regulatory scope and preemption of state laws. Despite reintroductions like the bipartisan CREATE AI Act authorizing the NIST AI Safety Institute in late February 2026 and ongoing state-level momentum with over 1,500 AI bills introduced across 45 states by March, no major federal legislation has advanced to floor votes or committee passage in recent months. The Trump administration's March framework emphasizes light-touch executive actions and federal preemption, reducing urgency for sweeping congressional bills before the session ends in December 2026, though a late omnibus rider remains a low-probability wildcard.

markets.question: Will Trump visit China by May 15?
markets.description:
If U.S. President Donald Trump visits China by May 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the listed country. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will Trump visit China by...?
events.description:
If U.S. President Donald Trump visits China by October 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the listed country. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Donald Trump is scheduled for a state visit to China on May 13-15, 2026, to hold a summit with Xi Jinping in Beijing, marking his first trip there as president since 2017. US advance security and logistics teams arrived via C-17 aircraft from Joint Base Andrews in recent days, confirming preparations amid reports of business leaders like Elon Musk and Tim Cook joining the delegation. The agenda focuses on bilateral talks covering trade tariffs, Taiwan arms sales, artificial intelligence cooperation, semiconductors, and China's ties to Iran during ongoing regional tensions. Traders should monitor for potential last-minute disruptions from escalating foreign policy issues, such as the Iran conflict or diplomatic setbacks, though official announcements signal firm commitment to the summit.

markets.question: Will Mike Kennealy win the 2026 Massachusetts Governor Republican primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Massachusetts, scheduled to take place on September 1, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Massachusetts Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Massachusetts Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Massachusetts Governor Republican Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Massachusetts, scheduled to take place on September 1, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Massachusetts Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Massachusetts Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Minogue's commanding 70% victory at the Massachusetts Republican Party convention on April 26, securing the official endorsement and advancing alongside Brian Shortsleeve while eliminating Mike Kennealy below the 15% delegate threshold, drives his 84.5% implied probability as the GOP gubernatorial primary frontrunner. Minogue's fundraising edge—$9.9 million cash on hand versus Shortsleeve's $732,000—powers statewide TV ads and digital outreach exceeding 15 million impressions, bolstering voter outreach in the low-turnout primary. Shortsleeve holds 16.4% amid calls for debates that Minogue has skipped, but trader consensus reflects the endorsement's weight in Massachusetts GOP dynamics ahead of the September 1 primary.

markets.question: UFC Fight Night: Alice Ardelean vs. Polyana Viana (Women's Strawweight, Prelims)
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Alice Ardelean" if Alice Ardelean is officially declared the winner of the fight against Polyana Viana at UFC Fight Night: Allen vs. Costa, scheduled for May 16, 2026.

It will resolve to "Polyana Viana" if Polyana Viana is officially declared the winner.

If the fight is declared a draw or technical draw, ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond May 30, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

events.title: UFC Fight Night: Alice Ardelean vs. Polyana Viana (Women's Strawweight, Prelims)
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Alice Ardelean" if Alice Ardelean is officially declared the winner of the fight against Polyana Viana at UFC Fight Night: Allen vs. Costa, scheduled for May 16, 2026.

It will resolve to "Polyana Viana" if Polyana Viana is officially declared the winner.

If the fight is declared a draw or technical draw, ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond May 30, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alice Ardelean (11-7-0, 2-2 UFC) meets UFC veteran Polyana Viana (13-8-0) in a women's strawweight prelim at UFC Fight Night: Allen vs. Costa on May 16 at the Apex in Las Vegas. Ardelean rides a two-fight UFC win streak via unanimous decisions over weak opposition, boosted by high-volume striking (138 and 150 significant strikes landed in her past two bouts) after switching camps to Fusion Xcel in Florida for better output and takedown defense. Viana enters on a three-fight skid marked by dismal striking volume—roughly 30 significant strikes across those losses—and must leverage her Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu (eight submission wins) against Ardelean's upright style. No recent injuries or withdrawals reported, with weigh-ins pending; stylistic edges favor the active striker in trader consensus.

markets.question: CA Osasuna vs. Club Atlético de Madrid: O/U 8.5 Total Corners
markets.description: In the upcoming La Liga game between CA Osasuna and Club Atlético de Madrid, scheduled for May 12, 2026 at 3:30 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Over" if there are 9 or more total corners awarded in this game (combining corners for both teams). If the total number of corners is fewer than 9, this market will resolve to "Under". If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50. This market refers only to corners awarded within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Corners awarded during extra time or penalty shootouts do not count. The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: CA Osasuna vs. Club Atlético de Madrid - Total Corners
events.description: Total corners markets for the La Liga game between CA Osasuna and Club Atlético de Madrid, scheduled for May 12, 2026 at 3:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Osasuna's La Liga matches average 8.24 total corners this season, with home games dipping to 8.00 amid their mid-table position (10th, 42 points) and recent mixed form featuring no clean sheets in six outings. Atlético Madrid, fourth with 63 points, boasts the third-highest corner generation at 6.29 per game but faces a depleted squad for this Estadio El Sadar clash, missing Julián Álvarez, Pablo Barrios, Johnny Cardoso, Nicolás González, and Giuliano Simeone through injury, alongside Osasuna's suspended goalkeeper Sergio Herrera. Head-to-heads show Atlético dominance (19 wins in 23) but low BTTS (26%), suggesting controlled play; late-round stakes could spur more set pieces from Atlético's away pressing.

markets.question: Solana Up or Down - May 13, 1:30AM-1:35AM ET
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Solana price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the SOL/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/sol-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream SOL/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Istanbul be 17°C on May 13?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by NOAA at the Istanbul Airport in degrees Celsius on 13 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=LTFM

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects close alignment between major forecast models like GFS and ECMWF, which project a high temperature of 20-21°C at Istanbul Airport (per NOAA measurements) on May 13, driven by a cooling trend following recent highs near 24°C on May 11 amid northerly flow and partial cloud cover. Mid-May climatology supports this range, with historical averages around 21°C moderated by Marmara Sea breezes and light winds limiting peak heating. The tight 35% (21°C) vs. 33.5% (20°C) split underscores uncertainty in exact cloud timing and boundary layer mixing, which could differentiate outcomes by 1°C; new 12z model runs and hourly observations will refine probabilities before resolution.

markets.question: Trump declassifies new UFO files by May 15?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Trump administration declassifies any files pertaining to extraterrestrial life and/or unexplained aerial phenomena which were not previously publicly available by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For purposes of this market, the “Trump administration” includes the Executive Office of the President and all executive branch departments, agencies, and subordinate offices under presidential authority during the Trump presidency, including the Department of Defense and its components.

Announcements of declassifications that are not implemented within this market's timeframe will not count.

The primary resolution source for declassification will be official information from the government of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

This historic transparency push, spanning decades of sightings, reflects bipartisan congressional pressure and fulfills campaign pledges amid heightened public interest following prior hearings. Traders weigh ongoing executive actions against definitions of "new" files, with no evidence of extraterrestrial origins confirmed yet; upcoming releases could shift probabilities before year-end deadlines.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Analilia Mejia's 20-25% margin of victory in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election, reflecting certified tallies showing her defeating Republican Joe Hathaway 60.2% to 39.4%—a 20.8-point edge—with all precincts reporting. This outcome stems from the Democratic-leaning district's history, where outgoing Rep. Mikie Sherrill (now governor) secured large wins, compounded by Democratic overperformance in recent House special elections and low special-election turnout favoring the progressive nominee backed by figures like Bernie Sanders. Pockets of softer support in Jewish-heavy towns like Livingston highlighted Israel policy divides, but did not dent the landslide. Minimal shifts from any remaining provisional ballots or final canvass pose the only realistic challenge, per historical New Jersey election patterns.

markets.question: Will Netflix, Inc. (NFLX) hit (LOW) $85 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Netflix, Inc. (NFLX) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Netflix, Inc. (NFLX) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.NFLX%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.NFLX%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

Q2 earnings on July 16 loom as the key catalyst, with May price action hinging on content hits and macro risk appetite.

markets.question: Arizona Diamondbacks vs. Texas Rangers
markets.description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Arizona Diamondbacks and Texas Rangers, scheduled for May 12 at 8:05PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Arizona Diamondbacks" if the Arizona Diamondbacks win the game.

This market will resolve to "Texas Rangers" if the Texas Rangers win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: No change in Bank of Japan’s interest rates after the July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The Statement on Monetary Policy for the Bank of Japan's Monetary Policy meeting for July is scheduled to be released on July 31, 2026 (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm).

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the short-term policy interest rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of Japan's July 2026 meeting.

If the short-term policy interest rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of Japan (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Japan's statement for the specified meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.
